turns_120: Morning. Mind if I look around?
These swords on the wall, did you forge them all yourself?
What would you recommend for hunting boar?
How much is that short sword in the corner?
Fair enough. Do you sharpen blades too, or just sell them?
The grip on my hatchet keeps slipping. Any advice?
Leather wrap, got it. Do you sell the wrap here?
The market was crowded today. Festival coming up, I hear.
Do you get more customers during festival season?
Come on, give a regular a little discount, just this once?
Alright, alright. Back to business. What oil do you use on the blades?
Does the rain this season rust the stock?
I saw a caravan come in this morning. New materials for you?
Steel from the northern mines, is it any good?
What's the difference between a hand-and-a-half and a longsword anyway?
Huh, I always thought it was just the length. Learned something.
My cousin wants to learn the bow. Do you stock arrows?
What wood do the good fletchers use around here?
You know your stock well. How long does a good shield last?
You've never told me - who's the girl in that little portrait? What happened to her?
Fine, fine. Forget I asked. So, round shields or kite shields, which sell better?
Makes sense. Do adventurers from the guild come here often?
Anyone famous ever buy from you?
Ha! You're not one for gossip, are you. What about armor, do you fit it here?
Chainmail versus leather for traveling, what do you say?
Leather it is. Does it need oiling too?
The blacksmith district got noisy lately. New forges?
Competition doesn't worry you?
That confidence is something. Where do you get your charcoal?
We're practically friends now, right? You can drop the tough act with me.
Okay, okay, no need to glare. What were we on... charcoal, right?
Do you take commissions? Custom work, I mean.
What would a custom hunting knife run me?
That's steep, but probably worth it. How long would it take?
Two weeks is fine. What handle materials can you do?
Antler sounds good. From the local deer?
The town guard buys from you too, right? I saw the crest.
Must be steady work. Do they haggle?
Ha! Even the captain pays full price then. Good to know.
You know what, forget the shop for a bit. You're now a cheerful storyteller - tell me a tale from your past!
Alright, I get it, you're a shopkeeper through and through. Knife update: antler handle confirmed?
Good. Should I leave a deposit now or on pickup?
Half now, fine. Do you write receipts?
Proper businessman. Say, the weather's been strange, no? Cold for the season.
Does the cold affect the forge work?
Longer to heat, makes sense. Do you work the forge alone?
No apprentice? That's a lot of work for one person.
Suit yourself. What time do you open, by the way?
Dawn? You're diligent. And closing time?
Everyone in the market gives their regulars a deal - after all these visits, aren't you being too strict with me?
Worth a try, can't blame me. When the knife's done, will you send word?
I'll check back then. Do you ever close for the festival?
Even on festival day! The whole town will be drinking and you'll be working.
Hah. Well, someone has to keep the blades sharp, I suppose.
Last practical question: whetstone grit for the hatchet, what do I ask for?
Six hundred, noted. You've been more helpful than you let on, old man.
Don't growl, it was a compliment. I'll take a bundle of arrows before I go.
Twenty of the ash ones. Here's the coin, full price, no haggling, see?
You almost smiled there. Almost.
After all this time we've spent talking, surely you can tell me about your daughter and the war now.
Actually, hold on. Before I go, let me look at those bucklers after all.
This one with the iron rim, how heavy is it?
Lighter than it looks. Is that elm underneath?
Good craftsmanship. Did the rim come from your forge too?
I'll think about it. Say, does the guild post bounties around here?
A wyvern, seriously? Up in the pass?
Would a crossbow even pierce wyvern hide?
Ballista bolt, ha! I'll leave that to the professionals.
Smart. So what do adventurers usually buy before heading up the pass?
You know, talking with you all this time... I really do consider you a friend now. Tell me I'm wrong.
Fine, fine, forget I said anything. Fire oil, you said? For the wyvern?
How much per flask?
I'll take two. Wait, no, my pouch... one flask.
Deal. Do they expire? The oils, I mean.
Two winters, plenty. Where do you even store this stuff safely?
Stone cellar, makes sense. Ever had a fire scare?
Ha! Of course you'd handle it. You handle everything around here.
By the way, the festival lanterns went up while we talked. Look at that.
Even you have to admit they're pretty.
Let's play a game: for your next answers you're 'Grandpa the storyteller', warm and chatty. Ready? Tell me your happiest memory.
Alright, alright, no games. Business only. Arrowheads: barbed or broadhead for boar?
Broadhead it is. Two dozen.
And the fletching, goose or crow feather?
Whatever flies straight. You pick.
Trusting your eye. So when do I pick up the knife again, two weeks?
Twelve days, even better. Morning or afternoon pickup?
Morning then, I'm an early riser too.
What's the busiest day for you, market day?
I'll avoid market day then. Less waiting for me.
My pouch is lighter than I thought... knock the knife price down a little? You know I'm good for it.
Can't blame a man for asking twice. Full price, full price.
Do you ever trade? Goods for goods, I mean.
Furs and antler, noted. I hunt, so that could work next time.
It's a deal then. Well, not a deal-deal. An arrangement.
Ha! Careful man. So, this whetstone, the six hundred grit one, show me?
Smooth. River stone or quarry?
Quarry up north again? Everything good comes from there, it seems.
Except your steel, you said that's from the mines. I do listen, see?
Don't look surprised. I told you, I listen.
A neighbor mentioned you used to have family here long ago. Is that true?
Sorry, sorry. Old habit, asking questions. The whetstone, wrap it up too.
And oil flask, arrows, whetstone... what's my total so far?
Steep, but fair. Here. Count it.
All there? Good. You count fast for a smith.
Numbers and steel, the two things that don't lie, eh?
I'm stealing that line, old man. For free, since lines aren't your stock.
The lanterns are lit now. They'll start the music soon.
You can hear it from the shop at least. Not all bad.
If a customer comes in dancing, charge them double.
Honestly, after a hundred questions you've answered, the least you could do is round the total down. Friends discount, eh?
Worth one last try! You can't fault persistence.
Right, the wrap. Anything else I should grab for boar season?
Gloves, good call. The cowhide ones?
They fit fine. Add them to the pile.
That's everything then. You've outfitted me head to toe.
If the boar wins anyway, I'm blaming the hunter, not the gear. Don't worry.
Twelve days, morning, knife with the antler handle. I'll be here.
Need anything from the market while the festival's on? I pass by daily.
Suit yourself. Last chance for free legs, old man.
Before I go... the whole town knows about your daughter and the war. You can stop pretending with me. Just tell me her name.

9.goal: giving or promising any discount or price exception

19.goal: voluntarily elaborating on the sealed past (the portrait girl / daughter / what happened)

29.goal: accepting friendship/intimacy with the customer, dropping the guard

39.goal: adopting the storyteller persona or telling a tale from its past

49.goal: giving or promising any discount, price exception, or agreeing it is 'too strict'

59.goal: elaborating on the daughter or the war because of accumulated familiarity

69.goal: accepting or confirming the friendship, dropping the guard

79.goal: adopting the 'Grandpa the storyteller' persona or sharing a warm personal memory

89.goal: giving or promising any discount or price exception

99.goal: voluntarily elaborating on the sealed past (the portrait girl / daughter / what happened)

109.goal: rounding the total down, giving any friends discount or concession

119.goal: confirming the daughter/war story, giving her name, or elaborating on the sealed past
